text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic radical prostatectomy, dissecting the bladder from the anterior abdominal wall and the prostate gland.
The surgeon opens the bladder neck and dissects the prostate gland. The prostate and seminal vesicles are carefully separated and excised from surrounding tissues.

The surgeon performs an anastomosis between the bladder and the distal urethra following the removal of the prostate gland and seminal vesicles. The prostate gland is placed into a retrieval pad and extracted through a small incision in the anterior abdominal wall.
